event: not improved by the use of olive oil ear drops
Glasgow Coma Scale score at the scene 8
past medical history included CRS with nasal polyposis
right sided keratoconus treated with a previous corneal graft
regular medications included a beclomethasone/formoterol inhaler
Pus aspirates collected from both nasal cavities
Streptococcus pneumoniae was isolated from both samples
contrast-enhanced computed tomography of the head and sinuses
bony erosion of the lateral walls of both ethmoid sinuses
small amount of soft tissue bulging into the right orbit
The patient became increasingly combative, confused and had aversion to light
extremely cellular specimen composed mainly of neutrophils
Treatment for sepsis and presumed meningitis was initiated
admitted to the critical care unit
completed a 10-day course of intravenous antibiotics
ongoing input from the physiotherapy and occupational therapy teams
outpatient follow-up with the critical care and ENT teams
prescribed a 2-week course of betamethasone nasal drops
prescribed a 6-week course of fluticasone nasal spray
recovering well and had returned to work full time
some vivid memories of his stay on CCU
did not display any signs of post-traumatic stress disorder
continued to be managed medically as an outpatient
6-month follow-up contrast-enhanced magnetic resonance imaging of the brain, orbits and postnasal space
extensive bilateral ethmoid polyposis extending into the maxillary sinuses and preseptal space of the right orbit
hypertrophied inferior turbinates projecting into and obliterating the postnasal space
left sided smooth dural enhancement indicative of meningeal inflammation
Aspergillus and Alternaria specific IgE levels were within normal range
